content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 907 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

July 28, 1976

Mr. John H. Goodwin
Deputy Commissioner III
Department of Real Estate
714 P Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Goodwin:

Thank you for your memorandum of July 1, 1976, expressing general objection to your Department's proposed Conflict of Interest Code.

With regard to any specific objections you may have, I would urge you to express them, in written form, directly to your Department, to permit them to be considered fully before your Department's Code is given final form. As you may not know, 2 California Administrative Code §18750(d)(3) requires the proposed Code from your Department to be accompanied by a copy of any written submission that you make. Accordingly, any comments that you submit will be forwarded to the Commission for its consideration. Further, pursuant to the provisions of 2 Cal.Adm. Code §§18750(h) and (o), you have the right and the opportunity, when the Commission holds a public hearing on your Department's Code, to appear and present your statements or arguments orally or in writing.

As you, perhaps, know, however, all deadlines for submission of proposed Conflict of Interest Codes by state agencies have been suspended as a result of the Commission's decision to reconsider several definitional provisions of the regulations and other issues relevant to formulation of Conflict of Interest Codes.

Sincerely,

Edgar A. Kerry
Staff Attorney
Conflicts of Interest
Division

EAK/gb

State of California

Memorandum

To : Fair Political Practices Commission Attn
       Attention: Ed Kerry

Date : July 1, 1976

File No.:

From : Department of Real Estate

Subject: Proposed Conflict of Interest Code of the
         Department of Real Estate and the
         Real Estate Commission

The Department has circulated an 18 or 19-page proposed code to its
employees. I believe a hearing was held on this in Los Angeles on
June 29th but I haven't heard anything about that. A hearing is to
be held here in San Francisco on July 6th.

Several employees in this office, including me, have written to
the head of our Department voicing our objections. Some others who
have not written have told me they too object. Following is a the
text of my memo(our Xerox isn't working):

"TO: John S. Hampel           July 1, 1975
    Chief Deputy Director
    Sacramento Office

    Proposed Conflict of Interest Code
    of the DRE

    ________________________

    "I object to the disclosure requirements. I believe in a
    strict code but the disclosure requirements seem unreasonable
    and offensive. In a way it seems insulting to propose such disclosure,
    like asking an innocent employee to take a lie detector test."

    [signature]
    John S. Goodwin
    Deputy Commissioner III"

I would have nothing to report except perhaps a house that my wife owns,
inherited from her family, that she rents to our son. It is not practical
for me to try to study Government Code Section 3700 and pick up ideas, if
any, (legally) in the proposal. Deputies(investigators, actually)for a quasi-
law enforcement agency are placing themselves in a more vulnerable position
by making personal information available to those we regulate, many of whom
are known by us to have been incarcerated for crimes(felonies)and many of
whom have personal enmity for the Deputy who caused the revocation of their
license(s).

(2)

From my 20 years with the Department, it has seemed to me that the big
conflicts of interest start at or close to the Assistant Commissioner
level. I know of no financial interest(in recent years)but the conflict,
in my opinion, is the close association with those we regulate. This
leads to a year-after-year first name basis association. Also, attorneys
who are legislators, and the legislators are sort of our "bosses over all",
represent individuals against the department. These legislators would
not, in my experience, influence grades below Deputy Commissioner V(Assistant
Commissioner).

[Signature]
John H. Goodwin
Senior Deputy
